title: Eeramana Rojave Lyrics Ilamai Kalangal
stanzas:
Eeramaana rojave
ennai paarthu moodaathe
kannil enna sogam
podhum ,yaengaathe
en anbe yaengaathe (2)

ennai paarthu oru megam
jannal saaththi vittu pogum(2)

un vaasalil ennai kolamidu
illai endraal oru saabam idu
ponnaarame

thanneeril moozhgaathu
kaatrulla pandhu
ennodu nee paddi
vaa sindhu.

Eeramaana rojave
ennai paarthu moodaathe
kannil enna sogam
podhum ,yaengaathe
en anbe yaengaathe

neram koodivantha velai
nee nenjai moodi vaitha kolai(2)

en nenjile ini raththamillai
kanneerukke naan
thaththupillai
en kadhali....

unpole en aasai
thoogaathu raani
kanneeril thallaaduthe
thoani.

eeramaana rojave
yaekkamenna rajave
kannil enna sogam
theerum yaengaathe
en anbae yaengaathe
yaengaathe
en anbae yaengaathe

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Eeramana Rojave'?

The song expresses feelings of love and longing, highlighting the pain of separation.

In which movie does 'Eeramana Rojave' appear?

'Eeramana Rojave' is featured in the movie Ilamai Kalangal.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by KJ Yesudas and S. Janaki.

Who composed the music for 'Eeramana Rojave'?

The music for this song was composed by Ilaiyaraaja.

When was 'Eeramana Rojave' released?

The song was released on August 19, 1983.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song 'Eeramana Rojave' from the movie *Ilamai Kalangal* beautifully encapsulates the emotions of love and longing. It conveys a sense of melancholy as the lyrics reflect on the pain of separation and the memories of a beloved. The soothing melody, paired with the heartfelt vocals of KJ Yesudas and S. Janaki, evokes deep feelings of nostalgia. This classic piece remains a favorite for those who appreciate the depth of Tamil music.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Eeramana Rojave from Ilamai Kalangal is a timeless melody that captures the essence of love and longing. With the soulful voices of KJ Yesudas and S. Janaki, this song resonates deeply with anyone who has experienced heartache. The enchanting composition by Ilaiyaraaja adds a layer of nostalgia, making it a must-listen for fans of classic Tamil music.
stanzaMeanings: Don't look at me, Eeramana Rojave.
Don't look at me, Eeramana Rojave. The sorrow in my eyes is enough, my love, where are you?
Looking at me, a cloud will pass by the window.
If you don't embrace me at your doorstep, this is a curse, it seems.
In the water, the wind doesn't blow, come to me, my dear, sing.
Don't look at me, Eeramana Rojave. The sorrow in my eyes is enough, my love, where are you?
The time that has come is a job that you have closed in your heart.
In my heart, there is no blood anymore, I am not crying, my love....
Like you, my desire doesn't fade, the queen doesn't shed tears in the water.
Eeramana Rojave, what should I ask, my king? The sorrow in my eyes will not go away, my love, where are you?
